# Gmsh CLI oneshot

Wrap the `gmsh` CLI, feed it a tiny `.geo` model, and verify a mesh file is produced.

## Why This Case Exists

A lot of engineering tooling is still command-driven, so one-shot CLI ingestion has to feel just as first-class as Python package ingestion.

## Case Shape

- ID: `gmsh_cli`
- Tier: `gha_subset`
- Style: `command`
- Workflow Slice: `aerodynamics`
- Target Kind: `command`
- Upstream Target: `gmsh`
- Catalog Source: `challenges/cases/gmsh_cli/challenge.toml`

## Run This Case

Use the convenience target:

```bash
make challenge CASE=gmsh_cli
```

Or call the runner directly:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python scripts/run_challenges.py --catalog-dir challenges/cases --tier all --only gmsh_cli
```

## Fixtures

- `challenges/cases/gmsh_cli/fixtures/box.geo`

## Smoke Flow

### 1. Generate a mesh from the tiny Gmsh fixture

Tool: `run_gmsh`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "extra_argv": [
    "{challenge_fixture_dir}/box.geo",
    "-3",
    "-o",
    "{challenge_artifact_dir}/box.msh"
  ]
}
```

Expectations: json fields {'exit_code': 0}; files are non-empty ['{challenge_artifact_dir}/box.msh'].

## What This Case Proves

- A standalone engineering CLI can be scaffolded from live help output.
- The generated wrapper can pass through real command-line arguments deterministically.
- A raw upstream command can emit a non-empty engineering artifact under `mcpme` control.

## Challenge Definition

```toml
# Self-contained case: treat a meshing command like a real external engineering executable.
# The checked-in `.geo` file keeps the case tiny while still producing a real mesh artifact.
id = "gmsh_cli"
title = "Gmsh CLI oneshot"
tier = "gha_subset"
style = "command"
slice = "aerodynamics"

[example]
summary = "Wrap the `gmsh` CLI, feed it a tiny `.geo` model, and verify a mesh file is produced."
motivation = "A lot of engineering tooling is still command-driven, so one-shot CLI ingestion has to feel just as first-class as Python package ingestion."
proves = [
  "A standalone engineering CLI can be scaffolded from live help output.",
  "The generated wrapper can pass through real command-line arguments deterministically.",
  "A raw upstream command can emit a non-empty engineering artifact under `mcpme` control.",
]

[target]
kind = "command"
value = ["gmsh"]

[probe]
commands = [["gmsh"]]

[scaffold]
kind = "command"
function_name = "run_gmsh"
help_probe_args = ["-help"]

[smoke]
[[smoke.steps]]
label = "Generate a mesh from the tiny Gmsh fixture"
tool = "run_gmsh"
arguments = { extra_argv = ["{challenge_fixture_dir}/box.geo", "-3", "-o", "{challenge_artifact_dir}/box.msh"] }

[smoke.steps.expect]
json_fields = { exit_code = 0 }
files_nonempty = ["{challenge_artifact_dir}/box.msh"]
```
